personality: 1 iOS-Native Clean with Brutalist/Utility enterprise data density
personality_one_liner: A premium, tablet-first ERP designed with the effortless utility of Linear, the layout clarity of Stripe Dashboard, and the native elegance of Apple.

spacing.rule: Generous Apple-native padding on outer screen edges, tight utility-driven spacing inside data tables. Multi-column cards use flexWrap with space-between. Absolutely no half-width orphans. Use safe area at screen root.

do's: Use on sticky tablet sidebar headers
Use on active Quotation panel headers
Use on 'Confirm Quotation' sticky CTA container
Use on Customer Portal order tracking floating cards
dont's: Do not use inside data grids or tables
Do not use on standard form inputs
Do not use on product catalog cards

screens.name: Auth
screens.purpose: Role-based entry point to the ERP.
screens.layout: Tablet: Split screen layout. Left 50% uses full-bleed luxury bathroom imagery (hero_auth) with vertical gradient scrim and elegant typography (Forge). Right 50% centered minimal login form. Mobile: Standard vertical layout with image as top 30% header.

screens.name: Dashboard
screens.purpose: Role-based home overview focusing on actionable KPIs.

states.empty: Illustration of a clean desk with 'No recent activity'.
states.error: Error state in widget container with 'Retry' CTA.

screens.layout: Tablet: Custom sidebar spine (left 20%). Main area (80%) features top row of 3-4 KPI metric cards (Revenue, Pending Quotes). Below, 2-column layout: Left col 'Recent Activity' list, Right col 'Top Products' list. Use 0.5pt subtle borders.

screens.name: Product Catalog
screens.purpose: Browse and filter the entire distributor inventory.

states.error: Full screen error with retry button.

screens.layout: Left sidebar (spine). Main area top header: sticky glass search bar. Left 25% of main area: persistent filter list (Brand, Category). Right 75%: Grid of product cards (image top, text bottom). Multi-column flex-wrap space-between.

screens.name: Quotation Builder
screens.purpose: Keyboard-first, ultra-fast POS checkout and quote assembly.
screens.layout: Tablet: Split view. Left 60%: Instant catalog search + visual grid of products. Right 40%: The Active Quotation 'Receipt'. Shows line items with inline numeric inputs for Qty/Discount (use Mono font). Sticky footer in right panel: Live totals + 'Confirm Quotation' primary CTA pinned to bottom.

screens.name: Customer Portal Home
screens.purpose: Consumer-facing order tracking and document access.
screens.layout: Apple-native feel. Top hero banner with tinted brand image and gradient scrim. Below: Vertical list of 'Active Quotations' as large elevated cards (shadow 1). Floating glass container at bottom for 'Contact Support' actions.

hero_auth.alt: Minimalistic modern luxury bathroom interior with white sink and mirrors

hero_customer_portal.alt: Contemporary luxury bathroom with round ceramic bathtub

product_brass_faucet.alt: Modern brass faucet over sleek black basin

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly adhere to the '1 iOS-Native Clean' guidelines. The UI must feel like Linear + Stripe Dashboard — highly dense but meticulously organized with generous outer padding.
BANNED: Any use of blue, indigo, purple, or navy. Use the provided monochromatic Carbon/Graphite theme.

Typography: Use Geist for all standard UI. You MUST use a monospace font (like Space Mono or JetBrains Mono) for all numeric data (SKUs, pricing, quantities, live totals in the Quotation Builder) to create an authentic ERP/POS feel.
Tablet-First Navigation: Implement a custom permanent left sidebar ('spine') for tablet dimensions, falling back to a bottom tab bar for mobile sizes.

Quotation Builder is the flagship screen: it MUST be a split-pane layout on tablet (Catalog on left, receipt/ticket on right). Ensure sticky 'Confirm Quotation' CTA at the bottom right. Use KeyboardAvoidingView perfectly here.
Every hero/background image MUST have a top-to-bottom vertical gradient scrim (transparent to surfaceInverse at >=70%) to ensure text readability.
Implement haptics precisely as mapped in the haptics block.
